methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

data.narrative:
Luma AI, an AI & Software company at Series C stage, commands a current post-money valuation of $4.0B after raising $1.07B to date. The peer universe consists of 9 comparable companies—1 public (PLTR) and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage. Key private comparables include Figure AI ($39.0B), Skild AI ($14.0B), Neuralink ($9.7B), ElevenLabs ($3.0B), and Runway ($1.5B).

Two valuation methods triangulate to a consistent range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $6.8B. Peer-median analysis across the 6-company same-sector, same-stage cohort produces $6.3B, with the peer set spanning $1.5B to $39.0B. These methods converge tightly, with a median implied value of $6.6B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $5.4B (low) to $7.8B (high), with a median of $6.6B. This represents a 65% uplift from the current $4.0B valuation. The peer-median range reflects material dispersion—$37.5B between minimum and maximum—indicating that sector and stage alone do not fully constrain valuation. Key uncertainties include differentiation within the AI & Software cohort, revenue traction relative to peers, and capital efficiency metrics not present in this dataset.
